items.title: Shapoorji Refinancing kickoff eases investor debt concerns
items.summary: Separately, the group raised $650 million through a three-year dollar bond on Friday at a yield of 14.5%, other people said

items.title: Korea’s SK Hynix shares make stellar US market debut, rocket 13% on continued AI optimism
items.summary: SK Hynix's U.S. shares surged thirteen percent on their Nasdaq debut. The South Korean chipmaker raised twenty-six point five billion dollars through a share sale. This demonstrates continued investor interest in AI-linked semiconductor stocks. Recent market pullbacks have not deterred this strong sector appetite. The company's listing provides funding for new factory construction.

items.title: SBI MF IPO vs ICICI AMC: Which is the better bet for investors ahead of Rs 11,600 crore issue?
items.summary: SBI Funds Management's IPO offers investors a chance to evaluate its market leadership. The company's valuation appears reasonable when compared to its listed peers. SBI MF leads in scale and cost efficiency, while others excel in revenue generation. The mutual fund industry is experiencing structural growth, benefiting from increased savings. Investors with a long-term horizon may find this IPO attractive for its risk-reward.

items.title: IPO Calendar next week: SBI MF among 3 new issues to raise Rs 11,980 cr
items.summary: India's primary market anticipates a significant IPO rush totaling nearly eleven thousand nine hundred eighty crore rupees next week. SBI Funds Management and Alpine Texworld will lead the mainboard segment with substantial fundraising efforts. SBI Funds Management aims to raise eleven thousand six hundred ninety three crore rupees through an offer for sale.

items.title: Greed and Fear report: Christopher Wood sees 'AI fatigue' setting in. Why Jefferies is turning bullish on India
items.summary: According to Christopher Wood of Jefferies, AI-linked stock momentum is waning as investors discuss 'AI fatigue.' The firm prefers India's undervalued market over overhyped AI stocks, while remaining positive on the long-term AI investment cycle and memory chip manufacturers as beneficiaries.

items.title: Will Google be Berkshire CEO Greg Abel's best stock pick after predecessor Warren Buffett's Apple bet?
items.summary: After taking over the reign as Berkshire Hathaway CEO in January this year, Greg Abel began putting the company’s record cash pile of nearly $400 billion to work. At the centre of this shift in strategy is Google-parent Alphabet.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI alleging trade secret theft, says scheme was 'at every level'
items.summary: The two companies entered into a high-profile partnership in 2024 when ChatGPT was integrated into the iPhone's operating system.

items.title: TCS, HDFC Bank, 8 others with up to 49% upside. How many in your portfolio?
items.summary: In a recent report, the brokerage has shortlisted 10 blue-chip stocks across multiple sectors that it believes offer attractive value and the potential to deliver returns of up to 49% return. Here’s the full list.

items.title: 5 world market themes for the week ahead
items.summary: TSMC's earnings will offer insights into AI chip demand this week. Wall Street banks are set to report, with expectations for strong corporate profits. Oil prices remain volatile amid tensions with Iran and Strait of Hormuz flows. US inflation data will gauge the Federal Reserve's stance on interest rates. Chinese economic figures will reveal the impact of global events on its growth.

items.title: Wall Street Week Ahead: Investors to grapple with packed week of earnings, CPI, Iran headlines
items.summary: Upcoming economic data and corporate earnings will test the resilient U.S. stock market. Major banks begin second-quarter earnings season, which is expected to be strong. Key economic reports, including the consumer price index, are due next week. Oil prices jumped amid concerns over renewed attacks on shipping and global supplies. Investors seek rate-path signs from inflation data and geopolitical developments.

items.title: Fed officials fret over inflation risk, weigh rate hikes
items.summary: Federal Reserve officials remain concerned about persistent inflation pressures. Incoming price reports will guide future interest rate decisions by policymakers. A divided committee discussed scenarios for potential policy responses. Some officials favor holding rates steady while others see hikes as appropriate. The central bank awaits crucial economic data to shape its next move.

items.title: SK Hynix shares jump in marquee US debut as AI euphoria persists
items.summary: SK Hynix's Nasdaq debut saw its shares jump significantly after a large share sale. This event signals strong investor enthusiasm for chip stocks amid the AI revolution. The company's offering was oversubscribed, meeting strong demand for AI-memory themes. SK Hynix is a leading maker of essential high-bandwidth memory chips for AI. This listing aims to close valuation gaps and provide access to a larger investor pool.

items.title: German luxury carmakers split on hybrids as EV sales surge
items.summary: BMW dismisses hybrids as costly and unnecessary, while Mercedes is betting on them to ease the shift away from petrol and diesel.

items.title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 13 July 2026
items.summary: The Indian stock market saw significant gains on July 10, with Sensex up 1.08% and Nifty 50 rising 1.02%. Investor confidence grew from strong earnings reports and positive international developments, despite a minor weekly dip in indices after four weeks of gains.

items.title: SBI Funds raises Rs 1,655 crore in a pre-IPO placement
items.summary: SBI Funds Management secured ₹1,655 crore from investors before its IPO. This pre-IPO share sale occurred ahead of the company's upcoming public offering. The asset manager's ₹11,693 crore IPO is the largest public issue scheduled for 2026. PI Opportunities Fund-II and Enam's Akash Manek Bhanshali were among the significant investors. The transaction involved selling a 1.42% stake at ₹574 per share.

items.title: India, New Zealand adopt Strategic Partnership Roadmap to 2030 with trade target of NZ$7 billion
items.summary: India and New Zealand established a Strategic Partnership, aiming for enhanced cooperation. They adopted a 2030 roadmap to guide joint actions over the next four years. Both nations agreed to double bilateral trade to NZD 7 billion by 2030. The leaders welcomed the signing of a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement. Cooperation will expand across trade, agriculture, and innovation sectors.

items.title: Groww faces technical glitch, client withdrawals hit
items.summary: Groww experienced a temporary disruption affecting client fund withdrawals on Friday. This incident was attributed to an issue with its cloud service provider, Google Cloud Platform. Some users also reported difficulties depositing money into their accounts. The brokerage stated the problem was promptly resolved before market closing. Customers could then access their funds after the disruption ended.

items.title: IT rally powers markets over 1%; Sensex, Nifty end week with marginal losses
items.summary: Indian equity indices saw a rise on Friday, boosted by information technology stocks. Tata Consultancy Services' earnings met expectations, easing sector concerns which helped the market. The benchmark indices, however, finished the week marginally lower, ending a winning streak. Renewed West Asia tensions kept investor optimism in check earlier in the week. Brent crude futures traded above $76 a barrel on Friday evening.

items.title: Russia's diesel export ban deepens global supply crunch
items.summary: Russia's diesel export ban has significantly impacted global energy markets. This action exacerbated existing fuel shortages and drove prices higher worldwide. European gasoil futures reached record highs against Brent crude oil. The United States also saw diesel futures jump substantially on Wednesday. These events highlight the interconnectedness of global oil supply chains.

items.title: Three PSU stocks with strong expansion plans
items.summary: Here is a list of the PSU stocks with strong expansion plans and a growing order book.

items.title: The week in charts: Slow kharif sowing, capex caution, clean energy milestone
items.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.

items.title: Trump administration, senators advance sanctions bill targeting countries buying Russian oil
items.summary: US senators and the Trump administration have agreed on updated Russia sanctions legislation. This new bill aims to impose penalties on countries doing business with Russia. Senator Lindsey Graham met with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy in Kyiv on Friday. Zelenskiy underscored the need for increased sanctions pressure on Moscow. The legislation is expected to provide new tools to help end the ongoing conflict.

items.title: Plum picks Rothschild for a record $100 mn fundraise
items.summary: D2C beauty brand Plum is gearing up for its next phase of growth, tapping Rothschild & Co to raise $75-100 million for offline expansion and new product launches. If successful, the round would set a record for a standalone beauty and personal care brand in India.

items.title: Oil Ministry plays sunk cost card, mounts fresh E20 defence
items.summary: Defending its policy, the government warned of the risk of stranding massive investments made in ethanol infrastructure over the past few years. “If, after creating this capacity, we were to arbitrarily revert to E10, what happens to these investments?” the ministry asked.

items.title: The rains are winning: India's monsoon travel boom sends hotel bookings up 10-fold
items.summary: The monsoon season is attracting many travelers to various Indian destinations. Popular spots like Goa and Kerala are seeing increased tourist footfall this year. Hill stations in Himachal and Uttarakhand also report significant growth in hotel bookings. Overseas destinations like Tajikistan are experiencing a substantial rise in travel interest. Hotels and travel brands are offering attractive deals to boost seasonal tourism.

items.title: Duty cuts put UK luxury cars in fast lane in India; prices may drop by Rs 1-3 crore
items.summary: Luxury car prices in India are set to decrease significantly soon. This change follows the India-UK free trade pact taking effect July fifteenth. Customs duty on fully imported cars will drop to thirty percent. This reduction applies to an allotted quota of twenty thousand cars annually. Most companies are still preparing to announce their new pricing structures.

items.title: Trump admin eases export controls for UAE; Warren blasts 'corrupt' provision
items.summary: The Commerce Department will favorably review exports involving MGX, which used a stablecoin linked to President Trump's family for a $2B Binance investment.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI, alleges ChatGPT maker stole trade secrets to build AI hardware
items.summary: Filed in a federal court in California, the lawsuit alleges that OpenAI and two former Apple employees engaged in a coordinated effort to misappropriate confidential information related to Apple's hardware development.

items.title: SK Hynix rises 13% in Nasdaq debut. Chairman tells CNBC 'demand is enormous'
items.summary: SK Hynix has soared to a trillion-dollar market cap by serving some of the biggest names in technology, including Nvidia and Apple.

items.title: SK Hynix’s US shares jump 13% on Nasdaq debut
items.summary: South Korean memory chipmaker’s US-listed stock price rose as high as $177 after pricing at $149

items.title: US stocks today: US stocks end higher as investors turn to earnings season
items.summary: US markets ended higher with the S&P 500 near record levels, driven by SK Hynix’s strong Nasdaq debut and renewed momentum in AI-linked chip stocks. Investors remain cautious ahead of earnings season and inflation data, while geopolitical tensions and interest rate uncertainty continue to shape broader market sentiment.

items.title: Stablecoin issuer Circle just got the greenlight to operate as a bank. The shares are up 5%
items.summary: Stablecoin issuer Circle surged in premarket trading after the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency granted it approval to operate as a trust bank.

items.title: Bezos-backed fusion start-up to become first to go public
items.summary: General Fusion’s ‘steampunk’ approach to the technology is under scrutiny ahead of Nasdaq debut

items.title: Trai pushes back on spam tag for 1600, 140 numbers
items.summary: Trai clarified that tagging and blocking calls from 1600 and 140 series numbers is not allowed. Subscribers can manage promotional calls from registered telemarketers using the Do Not Disturb registry. The 1600 series numbers are designated for service and transactional calls by financial sector entities. This clarification follows Truecaller's statement about not marking such calls as spam. Spam calls from these number series have reportedly surged significantly.

items.title: iPhone 17 faces supply crunch as consumers rush to beat possible price hikes
items.summary: iPhone 17 models are experiencing shortages across retail and online platforms. This surge is driven by expectations of price increases and reduced supply. Retailers are actively informing consumers about potential upcoming price hikes. Market trackers note increased demand for iPhone 17 models in the current quarter. Apple has recently increased prices on other product lines.

items.title: PM Modi begins New Zealand visit, trade talks in focus
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived in Auckland for a historic visit to New Zealand. This marks the first Indian prime ministerial visit in four decades. Talks will focus on strengthening economic and trade engagement between the nations. New Zealand expects significant business benefits from the new free trade agreement. The visit aims to deepen people-to-people connections with the Indian diaspora.

items.title: IMFA plans shift in production site to save Rs 100 crore in costs: Company aims for output of 400,000 tonnes in current fiscal year
items.summary: Indian Metals and Ferro Alloys (IMFA) is set to achieve substantial cost reductions and boost its production. By relocating operations from Therubali to Kalinganagar, the company aims for a remarkable output increase of nearly ninety percent over the next two years. The Therubali facility will repurpose to accommodate a new ethanol production unit, enhancing overall operational capabilities.

items.title: Forex reserves rise $7.3 billion as FCNR & ECB flows pick up
items.summary: The country's stockpile of assets denominated in foreign currencies rose $7.26 billion to $674 billion in the week ended July 3, the latest data from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) showed. Of the total reserves, foreign currency assets increased $4.5 billion to $545.5 billion, while gold reserves increased $2.6 billion to $105.2 billion during the week.

items.title: 'What's in your shopping cart?' Govt plans first nationwide retail consumption survey
items.summary: The government is considering a nationwide retail consumption survey. This survey will gather granular data on consumer spending patterns. It aims to track retail sales of various goods across all business types. Digital payment data already shows robust spending across many retail categories. The ministry seeks to diversify official statistics with this initiative.

items.title: El Nino effect: Oilseed imports surge in April
items.summary: India significantly increased oilseed imports in April, driven by El Nino fears. Soybeans constituted ninety-five percent of these substantial oilseed purchases. This surge followed a domestic production decline and made imports commercially attractive. Industry experts noted the unusual jump in imports due to higher domestic prices. India relies heavily on imports for its edible oil requirements.

items.title: GST law panel clears proposal to shield buyers' input tax credit from supplier defaults
items.summary: A GST Council proposal will protect buyers from losing input tax credit. This protection applies if suppliers fail to deposit taxes with the government. Buyers' credit will be safe if invoices are reported and payments are made through banking channels. Tax authorities will pursue defaulting suppliers for recovery instead of penalizing purchasers. This significant shift addresses a long-standing industry demand and is expected soon.

items.title: Road map unveiled for availing duty sops on vehicle imports under UK trade deal
items.summary: India has notified importers on seeking government approval for duty concessions. This agreement allows reduced import duties on automotive imports from the UK. Conventional engine passenger cars will see duty reductions with specific quotas. Electric and hybrid cars from the UK will gain duty-free access later. Importers must obtain certificates of origin and pre-purchase agreements.

items.title: AAIB final report in January, says Fadnavis
items.summary: Following the Ajit Pawar plane crash incident, authorities have stated that further actions will be taken only after a comprehensive report on the event is submitted and thoroughly reviewed. The investigation is ongoing, and all necessary details are being gathered to understand the cause and circumstances of the crash.

items.title: Indian Bank drops capital raising plan on lower ECL hit
items.summary: Indian Bank has cancelled its planned share sale to raise funds. The lender's expected credit loss impact is now estimated lower than previously calculated. This revised calculation means the bank will not need to raise additional capital. The bank's net profit rose ten percent year-on-year in the fiscal first quarter. Gross advances and deposits also saw significant year-on-year growth.

items.title: Fino Payments Bank June update: Deposits rise 11%, loan referrals surge 3.5x
items.summary: Fino Payments Bank reported an 11% year-on-year growth in deposits to ₹2,755 crore, with 3.1 lakh new accounts opened. Its loan referral business saw a 3.5x increase to ₹240 crore, but transaction throughput declined 35% due to the shift to UPI.

items.title: How the U.S.-Iran deal set the stage for renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran's contradictory interperations of the memorandum of understanding have led to renewed fighting over the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: ‘It’s all about the number’: how Apollo gazumped Castlelake’s easyJet bid
items.summary: Takeover battle for UK budget airline took a last-minute twist after US investment giant outbid rival

items.title: Gold, silver prices today: Comex extends weekly losses amid renewed Middle East tensions
items.summary: Precious metals saw volatility on July 10, with COMEX gold and silver futures declining due to renewed Middle East tensions and concerns over rising interest rates. Gold fell to an intraday low of $4,081, while silver dropped to $59.25, both recording weekly losses.

items.title: Govt, industry brainstorms on ways to achieve $150 bn exports target for electronics
items.summary: Deliberations were held with industry stakeholders on achieving the USD 150 billion electronics exports target. India's policy framework must provide predictability and stability for global value chains to expand. Participants emphasized integrating MSMEs into global value chains to enable scaling as suppliers. Harmonization of HS Codes and closer coordination with Customs authorities were also discussed.

items.title: SK Hynix shares jump 14% in debut on US stock market riding on AI wave
items.summary: SK Hynix shares surged on their Nasdaq debut, showing strong investor interest in chip stocks. The company's offering attracted significant demand, exceeding expectations for memory chip market strength. This listing provides SK Hynix direct access to a large investor pool for future expansion. The firm leads in high-bandwidth memory chips essential for AI processing. Analysts anticipate continued capital expenditure growth in global cloud and AI infrastructure.

items.title: The superpower of public equity
items.summary: Liquidity is tempting companies back to the public markets — but it is not available to all

items.title: Railway ministry approves Rs 206 crore for Kavach installation on 680 route km in Northern Zone
items.summary: Indian Railways approved Kavach Version 4.0 installation on 680 route kilometers. This safety system will be deployed on Rewari-Delhi and Shakurbasti-Bathinda sections. The project has been approved at a cost of Rs 206 crore. Kavach aims to prevent train collisions and signal passed at danger incidents. This deployment will strengthen safety and improve operational efficiency across these routes.

items.title: Rabi harvest blues: Key crops sell 3-8% below MSP as maize plummets 24%
items.summary: However, the season’s main oilseed crop, mustard, emerged as the winner.

items.title: Edelweiss MF stops fresh inflow in 7 overseas schemes
items.summary: However, existing SIP, STPs to continue in 7 overseas schemes

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank to pursue acquisitions alongside organic growth
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank plans to pursue acquisitions and organic growth. The bank recently acquired Deutsche Bank's retail portfolio in India. This move aims to enhance scale and customer reach for the bank. Kotak Mahindra Bank will also tap into India's economic expansion opportunities. A strong balance sheet and leadership talent support future growth strategies.

items.title: Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn as shares surge in bumper US listing
items.summary: The shares surged as much as 17% in their Nasdaq trading debut on Friday following the record-breaking foreign listing.

items.title: MF equity inflows jump 26% even as equity remains volatile
items.summary: SIP inflows stay above the ₹30,000 crore mark for the fourth consecutive month

items.title: Man nearly sucked out of window mid-air on Ryanair plane, passengers say
items.summary: The man's wife held onto his legs for around five minutes to stop him from being sucked out, officials say.

items.title: Govt defends ethanol blending, says programme saved Rs 1.9 lakh crore in forex
items.summary: The government defends its ethanol-blended petrol program amid concerns. This initiative has significantly boosted the sugar economy and farmer incomes. It has also saved India substantial foreign exchange and reduced carbon emissions. Maize is now the largest feedstock for ethanol production, increasing farmer realization. Extensive research confirms E20 fuel is safe for all vehicle engines.

items.title: Apollo’s profitability challenge: turn easyJet into Ryanair
items.summary: If the PE firm wants to raise revenue and cut costs at the airline, there’s room for manoeuvre

items.title: Investors sell longer-dated AI debt amid Big Tech borrowing spree
items.summary: Waning demand highlights scepticism over sector’s long-term profitability

items.title: Trump says U.S. to continue talks with Iran despite scrapped ceasefire
items.summary: The ceasefire signed last month came under serious strain in recent days as U.S. and Iranian forces exchanged attacks for consecutive days this week.

items.title: Major car firms found not to have installed emissions-cheating devices
items.summary: A High Court judge said some major car manufacturers did not install a device to cheat emissions testing.

items.title: Exide's Bengaluru lithium-ion cell plant to generate revenue from Q3: CEO
items.summary: Exide Industries expects its Bengaluru lithium-ion cell plant to generate revenue from the third quarter. The company has invested significantly and will invest more to complete the first phase of this project. Initially, the plant will supply domestically manufactured battery cells for electric vehicles and energy storage. Commercial supplies for stationary lithium battery applications are also targeted from the third quarter.

items.title: Contracting platform Zetwerk receives SEBI nod for IPO
items.summary: Final details, including valuation and issue size will be determined through the book-building process

items.title: SK Hynix's shares surge 14% in blockbuster US market debut amid AI frenzy
items.summary: SK Hynix's shares surged 14% in their Nasdaq debut after a $26.5 billion sale, reflecting investor enthusiasm for semiconductor stocks amid an AI boom.

items.title: New Fed task force members share Chairman Kevin Warsh's embrace of AI
items.summary: The three members of a task force advising the Fed on artificial intelligence are strong proponents of the technology.

items.title: Bank credit growth at two-year high at 18.6% as debt market turns costlier
items.summary: Bank credit expanded significantly in the fortnight ending June 30. Deposit growth also showed a healthy increase during the same period. This credit demand stems from both consumer and corporate sectors. Corporate entities are increasingly favoring banks over debt markets for funding. The gap between credit and deposit growth widened to 570 basis points.

items.title: Setting up of Bureau of Port Security reviewed
items.summary: Union ministers reviewed the Bureau of Port Security's progress on Friday. Only licensed private agencies and CISF-trained personnel will secure ports. Training for port security personnel will commence soon using existing infrastructure. A database of all security personnel will be created for the Bureau. Sagarmala Finance Corporation has sanctioned over Rs 10,700 crore in financing.

items.title: Circle wins licence for some US banking activities
items.summary: Shares in stablecoin company rise after regulator grants approval to operate as trust bank

items.title: Morgan Stanley warns AI chip rally may be running out of steam
items.summary: Morgan Stanley's Lisa Shalett warned semiconductor stocks may be overvalued as hyperscalers increasingly develop lower-cost proprietary AI chips. She expects slowing AI infrastructure spending and weaker pricing power to challenge chipmakers, despite continued investor interest and SK Hynix's Nasdaq debut.

items.title: Govt's ethanol policy backed by 4 years of research, engine damage fears are myths: Grain Ethanol Manufacturers Association
items.summary: Ethanol blending in petrol does not damage vehicle engines, according to GEMA President Chandra Kumar Jain. He stated that government policy followed extensive research and expert consultations over four years. Concerns about fuel efficiency are also addressed, with other factors impacting mileage significantly. India's grain ethanol industry is developing, focusing on improving plant efficiency and production.

items.title: Circle shares surge 13% on getting regulatory approval to set up US trust bank
items.summary: Circle secured final regulatory approval from the OCC for its national trust bank. This allows the stablecoin issuer to manage its own reserves and client crypto assets. The charter places Circle's bank under direct federal supervision by the OCC. Digital asset firms are increasingly seeking traditional financial services and banking licenses. Circle is the issuer of USDC, a stablecoin pegged to the U.S. dollar.

items.title: Global Markets: Chinese fast fashion retailer Shein finally wins approval for Hong Kong IPO
items.summary: Fast-fashion titan Shein has gained approval for its Hong Kong IPO after previous attempts were blocked. Although the company has faced strict regulatory examination and backlash in the US and UK markets, it seeks to rebound. Currently, investors project its valuation could be between forty to fifty billion dollars. This IPO represents a vital turning point for Hong Kong's vibrant financial ecosystem.

items.title: PFC raises USD 300 mn via bonds carrying floating interest rates
items.summary: Power Finance Corporation, a state-run entity, has triumphantly secured USD 300 million via bonds that feature floating interest rates. items.summary: Central Public Sector Enterprises must now use TReDS for MSME supplier transactions. This move aims to resolve delayed payment issues for small businesses. The Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises issued this notification on June 30, 2026. TReDS is an RBI-regulated platform for invoice financing by financiers. This ensures timely cash flow for MSME suppliers through approved invoices.

The updated policies impose restrictions on engaging in financial and political prediction markets. Specifically, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley have enacted firm prohibitions, while JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America have also established comparable limitations. items.summary: A weak monsoon poses risks to India's rural economy and farm incomes. Sectors like agriculture and microfinance face significant challenges from reduced rainfall. Lower crop yields could increase food prices and dampen rural demand for goods. Hydroelectric power generation may decline, impacting energy supplies. Despite these concerns, India's financial system is expected to remain resilient.

This visit aims to strengthen India's relations with these four nations.

The Indian prime minister also held summit talks with his Australian counterpart Anthony Albanese. They reinforced the vital role of the bilateral partnership in ensuring a peaceful Indo-Pacific region. The two nations sealed landmark pacts spanning civil nuclear energy and maritime security sectors. Discussions also focused on ramping up defence ties and economic cooperation agreements.
